name: Josiah's reform
notes: Kings and Chronicles both recount the reign of Josiah: the repairing of the house of the LORD, Hilkiah's finding of the book of the law, Shaphan's reading before the king, the king rending his clothes, the enquiry of Huldah the prophetess, the renewing of the covenant, the great Passover, and Josiah's death at the hand of Pharaoh-nechoh. The shared core runs nearly word for word in places, as when Hilkiah says 'I have found the book of the law in the house of the LORD.' The two accounts order the material differently: Chronicles gathers Josiah's purging of the high places and idols into his earlier years before the book is found (2Chr 34:3-7), while Kings sets the destruction of the idols at length after the covenant (2Kgs 23:4-20); each is a complementary vantage on the one reign. Kings keeps the fullest record of the cleansing of the land and the altar at Beth-el, and Chronicles keeps the fullest record of the Passover provisions and the lament of Jeremiah at Josiah's death; these unique portions are left unlinked. The difference in how the reforms are arranged in time is left as a matter for further study.

links.label: Josiah was eight years old when he began to reign
he did that which was right in the sight of the LORD and walked in ... the way of David his father
in the eighteenth year ... the king sent Shaphan the son of Azaliah
Hilkiah the high priest ... the silver which is brought into the house of the LORD which the keepers of the door have gathered
deliver it into the hand of the doers of the work that have the oversight of the house of the LORD
to buy timber and hewn stone to repair the house
because they dealt faithfully
found the book of the law in the house of the LORD
I have found the book of the law in the house of the LORD And Hilkiah gave the book to Shaphan
brought the king word again
gathered ... the money that was found in the house and have delivered it into the hand of ... the work
Hilkiah the priest hath delivered me a book And Shaphan read it before the king
when the king had heard the words of the ... law ... he rent his clothes
the king commanded Hilkiah ... and Ahikam the son of Shaphan ... and Shaphan the scribe
enquire of the LORD for me ... great is the wrath of the LORD ... because our fathers have not
Huldah the prophetess the wife of Shallum the son of Tikvah ... keeper of the wardrobe ... she dwelt in Jerusalem in the college
Thus saith the LORD God of Israel Tell the man that sent you to me
Behold I will bring evil upon this place and upon the inhabitants thereof
they have forsaken me and have burned incense unto other gods ... my wrath ... shall not be quenched
the king of Judah which sent you to enquire of the LORD ... the words which thou hast heard
Because thine heart was tender and thou hast humbled thyself before the LORD ... rent thy clothes and wept before me
I will gather thee unto thy fathers ... to thy grave in peace ... thine eyes shall not see all the evil
the king sent and ... gathered ... all the elders of Judah and ... Jerusalem
he read in their ears all the words of the book of the covenant which was found in the house of the LORD
made a covenant before the LORD to walk after the LORD and to keep his commandments ... with all their heart
Keep the passover unto the LORD ... in Jerusalem
there was not holden such a passover ... nor ... all the days of the kings of Israel
in the eighteenth year of king Josiah ... this passover was holden
Pharaoh-nechoh king of Egypt went up ... to the river Euphrates and ... Josiah went against him
came to fight in the valley of Megiddo
his servants ... took him ... in a chariot ... brought him to Jerusalem ... buried him in ... the sepulchres
